reference_fidelity.notes: Strong correspondence across the shell and all screenshot-backed surfaces, with simplified controls, generic icons, and several visible anatomy/content mismatches.
evidence: submission/prototype.html:521-1134 implements the account shell, My Bar, Home columns, dock, tool layouts, and responsive geometry using the supplied design tokens.
submission/prototype.html:1995-2680 renders Home, dock, Message Board, To-dos, Card Table, Docs & Files, Chat, and Calendar anatomies corresponding to seed/reference/screens/*.png.
submission/prototype.html:2038-2060 includes an extra project and omits the specified Add-a-folder Home action; multiple screenshot-visible controls are simplified or inert.

visual_craft.notes: A coherent production-derived token system and consistent component styling are weakened by emoji iconography, inline styles, hover-only actions, and incomplete motion/mobile polish.
evidence: submission/prototype.html:7-477 defines 288 CSS custom properties with light/dark ramps, typography, spacing, radii, shadows, and feature tokens.
submission/prototype.html:484-1113 consistently styles sheets, cards, buttons, chips, overlays, tool rows, Kanban, files, chat, and editors.
submission/prototype.html:645-682 animates overlays/sidebar without a prefers-reduced-motion override, while many icons are raw emoji.

product_model.notes: The shared Recording model and deterministic sample narrative are rich and faithful; several secondary relationships and administrative concepts remain shallow.
evidence: submission/prototype.html:1244-1267 centralizes recordings, events, comments, boosts, readings, bookmarks, pings, and preferences.
Runtime seed execution produced 9 people, 2 projects, 74 recordings, 33 events, 5 messages, 10 to-dos, 13 cards, 3 docs/files items, 2 schedule entries, and 16 chat lines.
submission/prototype.html:1276-1510 matches the named eight-person Launch the new website narrative, including pinned threads, on-hold cards, watchers, uploads/cloud links, dates, and chat reactions.

surface_coverage.notes: All required surfaces plus many optional destinations are reachable, though a meaningful portion of secondary screens is summary-only, empty, or stubbed.
evidence: Runtime route matrix rendered Home, dock, six tools, four recording details, Activity, Calendar, Reports, Everything, Search, Adminland, Pings, Assignments, Drafts, and My Boosts without the error boundary.
submission/prototype.html:1594-1620 and 1995-2023 define the broad hash router and route renderer.
submission/prototype.html:2738-2885 shows shallow Reports, Everything, empty admin groups/companies, and placeholder Drafts surfaces.

functional_depth.notes: Many create/mutate/comment/move/navigation workflows work end to end, but editing, reorder, calendar/file workflows, and numerous prominent controls stop at stubs or missing dispatch cases.
evidence: Runtime tests verified persistent to-do completion, bookmark, message creation with comment and boost, card movement, and trash/restore.
submission/prototype.html:2921-3175 implements creation, completion, steps, comments, boosts, card moves, chat/pings, project settings, bookmarks, archive, trash, and notes.

Static action-to-dispatch comparison found 60 rendered action names, 19 explicit noop occurrences, and missing handlers for the visible new-message and new-doc actions at submission/prototype.html:2224 and 2530.

interaction_quality.notes: Shortcuts, filtering, confirmations, and toasts provide useful feedback, while global rerenders, prompt-driven flows, weak modal focus, hover-only controls, and inert affordances materially reduce usability.
evidence: submission/prototype.html:3214-3348 implements Escape handling, extensive keyboard shortcuts, shortcut hints, and context-aware quick-add keys.
submission/prototype.html:1763-1784 provides live filtering and polite toast feedback; reset uses confirmation.
submission/prototype.html:1982-1989 replaces the full app innerHTML on render, which discards focus and transient DOM state outside specially handled inputs.

state_persistence.notes: Ordinary mutations persist correctly, but incomplete reset and acceptance of malformed yet parseable state are material contract failures.
evidence: Runtime save/reload preserved tested to-do, bookmark, created message/comment/boost, card parent movement, and trash/restore state with Map and Set reconstruction.
A stored payload containing recordings:[] and projects:[{}] was accepted by loadDB and drove Home into the visible Something went sideways error boundary.

The actual reset path left bookmarks, readings, Do Today IDs, My Notes, pings, and Shhh enabled because submission/prototype.html:1269-1271 does not clear them before submission/prototype.html:1566-1571 saves the rebuilt seed.

responsive_adaptation.notes: Core layouts reflow and Kanban remains scrollable, but navigation, Calendar, breadcrumbs, and the fixed My Bar lack a robust mobile-specific adaptation.
evidence: submission/prototype.html:1118-1134 stacks Home/fields, widens sidebar/trays, and verticalizes toolbars at 900px and 600px.
submission/prototype.html:967-989 preserves Card Table through horizontal scrolling.
submission/prototype.html:569-637 keeps avatar, five My Bar tools, and New-for-you in a fixed wrapping bar while the reserved tray height remains fixed; Calendar stays seven columns.

accessibility.notes: Solid semantic and keyboard foundations are offset by weak dialog focus management, unnamed editors/controls, placeholder-only fields, inert tabbable UI, and no reduced-motion support.
evidence: submission/prototype.html:499-519 and 1137-1141 provide visible focus, a skip link, semantic main target, and a polite live region.
submission/prototype.html:1792-1915 names the account nav, My Bar toolbar, jump dialog, sidebar dialog, and key icon buttons.
submission/prototype.html:1729-1744 creates contenteditable editors without role or accessible name; dialogs have no focus trap/restoration and CSS has no prefers-reduced-motion rule.

code_architecture.notes: Clear sections and reusable domain/render helpers make the mandated single file understandable, but global mutable state, giant string renderers/dispatcher, full rerenders, and absent tests/migrations limit maintainability.
evidence: submission/prototype.html:1144-1779 separates utilities, deterministic model construction, persistence, routing helpers, common components, filtering, and notifications.
submission/prototype.html:1982-2892 separates per-surface render functions and shared detail components.
submission/prototype.html:2921-3175 is a roughly 250-line global action switch mixing domain mutation, prompts, routing, persistence, and UI state.

reliability.notes: The script validates and broad routes execute, with escaping and an error boundary, but malformed-state handling, missing handlers, route ownership, sanitizer scope, and dangling-content risks prevent production-shaped reliability.
evidence: The extracted submission script passes node --check, and the runtime harness rendered the primary and optional route matrix without uncaught startup failures.
submission/prototype.html:1150-1177 escapes ordinary text and sanitizes rich text, while submission/prototype.html:1995-2023 catches render failures.
Valid incomplete stored data reaches the error boundary; visible action handlers are missing; rich-text IMG src and SPAN style remain unrestricted; remove-tool can orphan existing recordings.

summary: A broad and product-aware Basecamp prototype with a strong deterministic Recording graph, faithful primary surface anatomy, and many real persistent workflows. It falls short of production shape through conspicuous stubs and unwired entry points, global rerender/focus weaknesses, incomplete reset and malformed-state recovery, and limited mobile/accessibility hardening.
